interactions.summary: Jiang defines the weights of the game-theory formula, explains energy as openness to being wrong, distinguishes conscious from subconscious coordination, argues conspiracies are weak, and applies the model to individuals, teams, and nation-states.

speaker_notes.note: Single-speaker lecture; no audience questions or quoted readings in this packet.

claims.claim: Coordination is the most important factor in the formula and is weighted at least three times as much as mass; energy is second and is weighted about twice as much as mass.
Mass should be measured not only as population but as cohesion: how much people like each other and will work together.
Energy is mental motivation, especially the costly willingness to say 'I am wrong' and commit to self-improvement.
Subconscious coordination is more powerful than conscious coordination because it avoids visible leaders, explicit plotting, friction, and loss of plausible deniability.
For one-on-one games, mass and coordination reduce to one, so the more motivated and self-improving person wins.
In team sports, mass is constant, so winning depends on energy times coordination: subconscious teamwork plus constant self-reflection.

The nation-state is the most powerful form of human organization, and Jiang predicts the United States, Germany, Japan, and Israel will dominate their regions over the next 10 to 30 years as the world becomes multipolar.

signature_moments.moment: The hardest energy is not digging a hole for twelve hours; it is saying 'I am wrong' in front of other people.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the three hardest words to say for a human being is, I am wrong
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This turns motivation from effort into ego death, making self-correction the hidden fuel of power.

signature_moments.moment: Subconscious coordination is 'almost like a dance': no order, no leader, but bodies moving together.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The dance image explains why Jiang thinks religion, ethnicity, and family coordinate better than committees.

signature_moments.moment: Conspiracy loses because people have egos; the meeting room itself creates friction, records, leaks, and cheating.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is Jiang's anti-conspiracy theory: power works best when nobody has to conspire.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Motivation measured by willingness to accept limitations, admit error, and commit to self-change.
Leaderless coordination in which people work together through shared identity, story, family, ethnicity, or religion.
Coordination led by an explicit leader, bureaucracy, or patriarch.

chronology_notes.note: This dated source contains a forward-looking regional dominance forecast for the next 10, 20, or 30 years from 2025-07-11.

uncertainty_notes.note: Transcript likely renders K. Anders Ericsson's 'deliberate practice' as 'through practice'; semantic pass preserves the intended reference with medium caution.
